sample.title: How the Iran War Is Rewiring the Oil Market
sample.description:
The world feared the Iran war’s disruption of the Strait of Hormuz would trigger historically high oil prices. Yet the worst hasn’t happened, and the reasons for that may hint at a rewiring of global energy flows.

That unexpected resilience of markets has many explanations, but it’s forcing a bigger question: does the world simply wait for the critical waterway to normalize, or start building alternative routes, inventories and supply systems to hedge against future risks?

Subscribe to Bloomberg’s Energy Daily newsletter, your guide to the energy and commodities markets that power the global economy, from journalists stationed around the world.
https://www.bloomberg.com/account/newsletters/elements

#Iran #Oil #Economy

sample.title: Why Space Companies Are Betting Big on Texas
sample.description:
Already home to SpaceX, Blue Origin and recent moon-landers Firefly Aerospace and Intuitive Machines, Texas is quickly becoming the center of the commercial space universe. Through a newly-formed agency, Texas officials have been doling out grants for space-related research, projects and emerging technology. So what does this mean for the "Lone Star State?" And why has it become such a proving ground for the startups of the future?

(Corrects map of Texas throughout video.)

SpaceX’s IPO to Mint Millionaires in Poor Texas Border Town: https://bloom.bg/4bsvut7

The company’s spaceport put Brownsville on the map, but locals fear being priced out by newly rich neighbors.

01:08 Firefly Aerospace sees success in Texas 
02:24 Why Texas appeals to the space industry
04:48 SpaceX history in Texas 
06:10 How the state of Texas is now wooing space business

sample.title: Why Prediction Markets Are So Hard To Regulate
sample.description:
Event contracts let people bet on outcomes ranging from sports and inflation to company milestones. But as these markets move closer to publicly traded businesses, old financial rules are being tested in new ways. CNBC's Ananya Chetia and Davis Giangiulio report on what this uncertainty could mean for platforms like Kalshi, Polymarket and others.

Credits:
Produced and shot by Charlotte Morabito
Reporting by Ananya Chetia, Davis Giangiulio
Edited by Andrea Miller
Animation by Alisa Stern, Jason Reginato
Senior Managing Producer - Shawn Baldwin

sample.title: Elon Musk’s Memphis Takeover — The Marvels And Messiness Of SpaceX’s AI Buildout
sample.description:
Elon Musk's xAI, now known as SpaceXAI following a merger with SpaceX, has made the Memphis, Tennessee area the center of its AI ambitions. XAI is building three data centers there, as well as a power plant in Southaven, Mississippi. XAI's AI infrastructure there truly deserves its name Colossus. It's a modern marvel that was built with breakneck speed. Anthropic and Google are now renting compute capacity at the data centers in a deals worth billions of dollars. But some say the buildout has been chaotic. XAI is now facing multiple lawsuits accusing the company of using loud, pollution-emitting gas turbines, some without the required Clean Air Act permits. CNBC traveled to Memphis and Southaven to speak with locals to learn why the data centers are such a flashpoint, to find out why backlash against data centers is spreading nationwide, and to learn what hyperscalers can do to win support as they race to build AI infrastructure.

Chapters:
00:00 - 3:54 Introduction
3:55 - 10:28 SpaceX’s AI ambitions
10:29 - 17:02 Gas-burning turbines
17:03 - 20:28 Constant noise
20:29 - 22:37 Millions of gallons of water
22:38 - 28:59 Growing AI backlash

sample.title: Consumer Prices See Biggest Drop In Six Years In June
sample.description:
The June consumer price index shows that annual inflation is moving closer to the Federal Reserve’s 2% target. Energy put downward pressure on inflation as a lull in the Iran war easing prices for consumers. CNBC’s Alex Harring breaks down what the June CPI reveals about prices.

sample.description:
Chinese automakers have made over $100 billion in EV and battery investments abroad since 2019, according to Atlas Public Policy, far outpacing US firms. CNBC’s Robert Ferris has more on how the Asian country got here and why.

sample.title: Economic outlook is worsening and Trump is getting blamed: CNBC survey
sample.description: Despite a booming stock market and improving inflation numbers, the public is as depressed about the economy as it has been since the years just after the pandemic and increasingly concerned about the cost of everyday goods, according to the latest CNBC All-America Economic Survey.

sample.title: AI-designed drone spins almost too fast to see
sample.description:
Researchers at Northwestern University have used artificial intelligence to design a drone that is far less visible in flight, exploiting motion blur rather than camouflage to make the aircraft appear as a faint haze.

sample.title: As the Commonwealth Games approach, is Glasgow ready to host? | BBC News
sample.description:
The Commonwealth Games gets underway in Glasgow in just under a week. Scotland's largest city stepped in after the state of Victoria in Australia dropped out.
 
It's had less than two years to prepare and will be hosting a scaled back programme of 10 sports - down from 17 sports in 2014.
 
BBC Scotland Correspondent Lorna Gordon has been speaking to people in the city about whether it's ready to host the games, and Sir Chris Hoy speaks to BBC Breakfast about the opportunities that the Games provide for Scotland.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

sample.title: Heartstopper Forever: Kit Connor and Joe Locke on queer joy, future plans and more | BBC News
sample.description:
Heartstopper has been praised since it first aired in 2022 for the way it has sensitively dealt with a number of topics that many teenage and young adult viewers will be familiar with - such as sexual identity, mental health and bullying.
 
The series is now coming to an end with Heartstopper Forever, a feature-length film.
 
And the two main protagonists, Kit Connor (Nick) and Joe Locke (Charlie) both even served as producers on the film.
 
The pair spoke to the BBC about queer love, acting as producers and whether they’ll work together again in the future. 

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#HeartstopperForever #KitConnor #JoeLocke #BBCNews

sample.title: US launches new strikes as Iran says civilian infrastructure hit | BBC News
sample.description:
The US launched a wave of strikes against Iran for the sixth night in a row, its military said, as the two sides battled over control of the Strait of Hormuz.
 
US Central Command (Centcom) said the attacks were intended to "further degrade Iranian military capabilities", before saying it had boarded a vessel as part of its blockade of Iranian ports.
 
State media and provincial authorities reported that the US had hit civilian infrastructure, including bridges, a train station and an airport. 
 
BBC Verify has confirmed an attack on one bridge to the west of Bandar Abbas in Hormozgan province.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#US #Iran #BBCNews
